subject: Statistics
title: How the FDA Approves Drugs
description: A fast breakdown of How the FDA Approves Drugs for builders.

hook.text: The FDA approves about 50 new drugs per year out of thousands submitted. The bar is deliberately high.

buildup.text: Approval requires proof of safety and efficacy from at least two independent Phase III trials.

discovery.text: An advisory committee of independent scientists reviews the data and votes on approval.

twist.text: The FDA sometimes disagrees with its own committee. Politics, patient advocacy, and urgency all influence decisions.

climax.text: The system is imperfect but essential. Without it, ineffective and dangerous drugs would flood the market.

punchline.text: High bar, slow process, lives saved. That's the trade-off.

quiz.question: What evidence does the FDA require for drug approval?
options: Proof of safety and efficacy from at least two independent Phase III clinical trials
A single successful trial with any number of patients
Only evidence that the drug is safe, not effective
